To evaluate personal purchases, first assign a reserve floor based on burn rate (3 months for normal, 6 months for income-linked, plus extra if dependent on shaky payments). Then assess payback: clean under 12 months, conditional 12–18 months, borderline 18–24 months, usually no over 24 months. Finally, check exit feasibility (2–4 weeks for vehicles, 30 days for common equipment, pass for custom/niche items unless cash-flow protection is clear). This structured approach enables a concrete, actionable decision. The corridor-specific decision rule is a literal yes/no checklist: (1) Is the primary route closed? (2) Is there a real alternate (I-10)? (3) Is there a 2-hour buffer or more? (4) Is the weather forecast clear or only light rain? (5) Are there known choke points (Beaumont, Orange, Lake Charles)? Answer 'GO' if yes to (1), 'DELAY' if no to (1) or (2), 'REROUTE' if (3) fails or (4) is light rain, 'CANCEL' if (5) is true and no buffer. User can work with the corridor checklist as-is.
